pi_items.source: docker-compose.yml volume mount specification shows /home/ga/.jitsi-meet-cfg/web:/config:Z; setup_jitsi.sh creates the directory at /home/ga/.jitsi-meet-cfg
Specified in task.json description and referenced in export_result.sh line 14 which checks for REPORT_PATH=/home/ga/fps_config_report.txt

Jitsi Meet documentation and community forums confirm desktopSharingFrameRate with min:15 is a valid and reasonable configuration for high-fidelity screen sharing. Zoom and other video conferencing platforms offer 15 fps as a quality option. Web search of 'screen sharing video conferencing high quality frame rate' confirms 15 fps is standard for quality screen sharing.

Jitsi Meet GitHub issues and community forums document desktopSharingFrameRate with max:30 as a configuration goal for smooth screen sharing. Web searches confirm 30 fps is standard quality for screen sharing (matching Zoom and Teams). Jitsi GitHub issue #5977 discusses 30fps desktopsharingframerate for smoother screen sharing.

Multiple Jitsi Meet community forum posts and GitHub documentation confirm default desktopSharingFrameRate is min:5, max:5. This is the baseline that the task sets to low values before the agent modifies it.
Jitsi Meet configuration documentation and forum discussions confirm enableLayerSuspension defaults to true. This feature suspends unused HD video layers to save bandwidth.

privileged_info_summary: The task configures Jitsi Meet screen sharing for high-fidelity video by setting desktopSharingFrameRate to min:15, max:30 fps and enabling layer suspension. The config file is located at /home/ga/.jitsi-meet-cfg/web/config.js and mounts to /config in the Docker web container. The default Jitsi settings are 5 fps with enableLayerSuspension already true. The 15-30 fps range is industry-standard for high-quality screen sharing, equivalent to Zoom and Microsoft Teams configurations. A verification report must be created at /home/ga/fps_config_report.txt.
